observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements within ten days from receipt of the bank statements and thereafter submit to COA for verification.  This is to ensure that deficiencies and errors in the previous month are immediately detected and eventually adjusted in the succeeding month to bring the Cash in Bank account to correct balance.  For this purpose, we suggested that the Chief Accountant prioritize the preparation of BRS by delegating some of his tasks to his staffs or assigning one of his personnel to exclusively prepare the BRS.  If budget would allow, we recommended that Management consider hiring additional Accounting manpower.

We commended the two Division Chiefs for their efforts in coming up with reconciled balance of the Cash in Treasury account.  Henceforth, continue reconciling regularly the GL and the cashbook to detect reconciling items for proper and timely adjustment thereof to show the correct balances of the two records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned personnel to comply with the preparation of records/reports prescribed under COA Circular 2015-008.  Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record immediately in the books of accounts the completed Tibo-Cagdarao Road Network costing ₱19.05 million.

We commended Management for submitting a complete RPCPPE for CY 2019 which was reconciled with the accounting records.   We also recommended that the agency keep up the commendable feat of coming up with a reconciled balance of PPE in the financial statements and RPCPPE.

We recommended that management enforce strictly the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances and discontinue the granting of additional CAs to accountable officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require concerned accountable officers with outstanding CAs to submit liquidation reports with complete and valid supporting documents for each kind of transaction.  Also, disbursements with material amounts should be paid by check supported with required documentary requirements.
We also recommended that CA shall be liquidated or settled as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to provide complete disclosure of the details not only of the Loans Payable account but also of all other accounts in the financial statements which are required to be adequately disclosed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify immediately the current portion of the long-term loan payable amounting to ₱3.63 under the same account but under the Current Liabilities portion in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all PPAs funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

We recommended that management prioritize the approval of the CLUP and revisit its CDP and ensure that climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction activities will be integrated in its land use plan.

We commended Management’s compliance with previous audit recommendation of facilitating the closure of the dumpsite.  However, we recommended that the Municipality cause the immediate construction of a temporary containment building and other required structures to ensure that residual wastes are properly handled and would not contaminate the ground pending the construction of sanitary landfill.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the attachments as required under Section 4.1.C.8.5 of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No, 2013-1 and provide sufficient details of the activities undertaken to facilitate validation of GAD programs and projects reported.
We recommended that the LCE require the Personnel-in-Charge of the preparation of JO Contracts to include the specific duties and responsibilities of the JO workers.

We recommended that Management keep up the efficient revenue collection. We also recommend that:
The Local Finance Committee:
Conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;
Conduct periodic review and monitoring of actual performance so that appropriate measures could be undertaken to achieve targets and further improve collection efficiency; and
Provide targets for accounts without projections based on statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectations.
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and other departments involved in generating revenues to intensify further collection of revenues that missed the targets.

We recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned to utilize the 20% DF in accordance with the implementing rules and regulations set forth in DILG memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure that the 20% DF is optimally used to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes envisioned for the fund.
We further recommended that in the planning phase, there should be thorough scrutiny of projects to be implemented under the 20% EDF making sure that projects included are practically viable to be implemented during the budget year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require: 

1.	The Accountant and Property Officer designate to complete the reconciliation process to eliminate the discrepancies in the inventory report and the accounting records;

The Property officer to complete the recording in inventory list all PPE items reflected in the Accounting records; and

The Property Officer to complete costing of properties.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:

1.	 Comply with pertinent rules and regulations on the recording of accounts payable and ensure that only valid claims are booked up; and
.
2.	Prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts in all Funds to reflect the correct balance of liabilities in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:

1.	 Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and

2.	Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We recommended that the LCE require the Local DRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that LCE direct the LDRRMO to be aware of the reportorial requirements of managing LDRRMF.  He shall prepare the annual LDRRMFIP to coincide with the budget preparation of the LGU, as basis for the LDRRMF budget to be incorporated in the Annual Budget of the LGU. Such LDRRMFIP shall include disaster risk reduction projects and activities, whether new or unimplemented, including other DRR management activities chargeable against previous years’ unutilized LDRRM funds.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare a closure and rehabilitation plan, submit the same to the EMB Regional Office for review and approval and cause the immediate closure of the LGU’s existing CDF and facilitate the immediate construction of a proper sanitary landfill.  Such immediate action is necessary to address if not prevent, detrimental ecological issues and their effects on human health and to the environment.
